whatThisIs: Pick the scenario that matches your situation - out-of-network emergency care, ancillary care at an in-network facility, air ambulance, or ground ambulance - and Sophie Well reports whether the federal No Surprises Act protects you and which statutory section governs. Source: Public Health Service Act Section 2799A-1 / 2799A-2 and 45 CFR 149.
whenToUse: Use this before paying a balance bill that looks like a surprise, before filing a complaint with the federal IDR portal, or when explaining to a provider's billing office why a bill is protected. State surprise-billing laws may add to (but not displace) the federal floor; consult your state department of insurance for state-level protections.
